name: RAPPstore — official
description: Public catalog of rapplications. Each rapplication is a converged single-file agent.py produced by the double-jump loop. Pull into your local brainstem's agents/ directory and it works. Optional .egg pack restores example workflow state.
install_protocol: drop the singleton .py file into your local brainstem's `agents/` dir. Then POST /api/swarm/deploy with a bundle that includes it, OR: simply hot-load it from disk if your brainstem watches the agents/ folder. The file is everything — no install script, no pip, no native deps.

rapplications.name: BookFactory
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor → CEO → Publisher → Reviewer). One call, one chapter back.
rapplications.tagline: Source material in, publishable chapter out — 8 LLM calls under one POST.
rapplications.egg_note: No pre-populated state .egg shipped — BookFactory is stateless per call. Output artifacts are written to the workspace dir specified per call.

rapplications.name: SpineDAG
rapplications.summary: Universal Directed-Acyclic-Graph analyser. Point it at any folder, code project, Excel workbook, JSON/YAML config, or block of prose; it builds the dependency graph, finds hubs, leaves, orphans, cycles, and the critical path, scores Spine Health 0-100, and returns Markdown with a Mermaid diagram, ASCII tree, and a <spine-dag-json> envelope a UI can render.
rapplications.tagline: Map the spine of anything in 30 seconds — code, Excel, JSON, prose.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless — no shipped state. Each call analyses a fresh target.

rapplications.name: WildhavenCEO
rapplications.summary: A CEO workspace agent. Drop a vault of strategy / legal / budget / pitch / playbook documents in, then ask questions and get answers in a confident operator's voice. Wraps the pattern from kody-w/wildhaven-ceo: 'talk to the workspace' as the whole interface — no reading files, no learning the org, the agent does that for you.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless per call. The workspace context is supplied at invoke time; no shipped state.

args.question: How are the rentals doing this month?
args.workspace_context: (paste your latest property-summary CSV + budget notes here)

rapplications.name: Vibe Coding Loop
rapplications.summary: Ship batches of 10 single-file HTML demos to a Jekyll site via parallel sub-agents. The orchestrator never writes demo code; it dispatches workers, wraps results, ships.
rapplications.tagline: One paragraph in, ten working browser demos out — in parallel.

rapplications.name: Cockpit
rapplications.summary: Local-first control plane for SSH-reachable hosts. Chat agent + HTTP backplane + browser UI on one local state dir. One operator drives N remote machines; nothing leaves the box.
rapplications.tagline: One terminal. N continuums. Zero leaks.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless catalog entry. Operator state (host inventory, audit log) lives in ~/.cockpit/ and is portable via the export_state / import_state actions.
rapplications.access_note: Source files live in kody-w/RAPP_Store_Private. Unauthenticated raw fetches return HTTP 404. With a PAT scoped for read on the private repo, every URL returns 200 and the rapp installs normally.

rapplications.name: Thoughtbox
rapplications.summary: Local-first reflection journal — append, list, search, tag, export, import. Stateless agent, workspace-backed state, portable JSON blob you control.
rapplications.tagline: A box for your thoughts. Local-first, portable, yours.

rapplications.name: AgentTeam
rapplications.summary: The persona-routing brain of Bill Whalen's Agent Team Starter Kit, ported to a single rapplication. Given a project goal, returns the outcome frame, persona route, paste-ready GitHub issue body, and the needs-you questions the team would surface.
rapplications.tagline: Plan a project the way the kit would.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless per call. Plans are returned in the response envelope; no shipped state.

args.goal: Build a Dataverse-backed intake form that routes customer feedback through Power Automate into a Copilot Studio bot, with a Power BI dashboard for the support lead.

rapplications.name: HatchProjectTwin
rapplications.summary: Hatch a project-anchored twin from the global RAPP brainstem kernel. Sibling to twin_egg_hatcher_agent.py (which hatches from eggs/repos/cwd); this agent hatches a fresh project-anchored twin straight from the global kernel. Files live in <project_path>/.brainstem/src/rapp_brainstem; a symlink at ~/.rapp/twins/<rappid-hash>/ makes the twin discoverable by the global brainstem's built-in Twin agent. Spec-compliant rappid.json, manifest.json, HATCH_RECEIPT.json.
rapplications.tagline: Project twin the global brainstem can manage. Same disk layout, same schemas, same vocab as twin_egg_hatcher.
rapplications.egg_note: Stateless per call. Twin workspace is the disk artifact (~/.rapp/twins/<hash>/ → project anchor).

senses.name: haiku
senses.summary: Translates the response into a 5/7/5 haiku. Same meaning, different mode.

senses.name: tldr
senses.summary: Compresses the response to a single load-bearing sentence.

senses.name: emoji
senses.summary: Translates the response into a 3-7 emoji narrative — a tone fingerprint.

senses.name: eli5
senses.summary: Re-explains the response so a five-year-old could follow it.

senses.name: headline
senses.summary: Compresses the response into a punchy news-ticker headline.

publish_your_own.summary: Author a rapplication using the multi-file source pattern (see blog/89). Run the double-jump loop until convergence (typically 1-3 cycles per content shape). Collapse to a singleton via tools/build-rapplication.py. Submit a PR adding an entry to rapp_store/index.json.

install_protocol_senses: Drop the *_sense.py file into your local brainstem at rapp_brainstem/senses/. Auto-discovered each chat request, no restart. Senses are TRANSLATIONS of the main response into different modes of expression (Constitution Article XXIV).
gated_rapplications_note: This catalog supports gated rapplications (SPEC §11). Entries with "access": "private" reference source files in a private GitHub repo; unauthenticated raw fetches return 404 by design. Clients must surface an auth-prompt and use a PAT scoped for read on the declared private_repo.
